execution_pillars.description: Signed extension registry with provenance, attestation, and revocation enforcement.
execution_pillars.user_outcome: Operators can publish, verify, and revoke extensions with deterministic trust evidence.

execution_pillars.description: Node/Bun-to-FrankenEngine migration kit with deterministic behavior validation artifacts.
execution_pillars.user_outcome: Migration cohorts can prove equivalence, divergences, and remediation paths before cutover.

execution_pillars.description: Policy-as-code, audit export, and compliance evidence contracts for enterprise adoption.
execution_pillars.user_outcome: Enterprise operators can plug FrankenEngine into governance and audit workflows without bespoke glue.

execution_pillars.description: Ecosystem trust sharing and incident response through explainable trust-card APIs.
execution_pillars.user_outcome: Trust and incident knowledge can propagate across organizations and environments.

execution_pillars.description: Lighthouse adopters and public case studies that prove the ecosystem story in production.
execution_pillars.user_outcome: External proof points exist for adoption, migration, and operational improvement claims.

adoption_targets.description: Minimal-friction deterministic safe-extension setup workflow for new users.
Representative Node/Bun extension packs migrated with deterministic validation artifacts.
Public deployments documenting materially improved security and operational outcomes.

upstream_prerequisites.reason: Adoption artifacts need canonical evidence entries with trace-linked hashes.
High-impact safety actions must route through explicit decision contracts.
Public adoption claims depend on the benchmark specification and scoring methodology.
Migration claims require differential Node/Bun parity evidence.
Third parties need an independent verifier toolkit for benchmark and replay claims.
Trust sharing depends on low-latency reputation updates and explainable trust cards.
The reputation graph API needs a stable provenance and trust-transition schema.
Serialization, signature, revocation, and epoch claims need conformance coverage.

closure_rule.parent_epic_note: Closing bd-3bz4 advances Section 15, but bd-1jak still tracks other Section 15 outputs plus Sections 13 and 14 and master-program blockers.

operator_verification: rch exec -- env RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN=nightly CARGO_TARGET_DIR=$PWD/target_rch_ecosystem_capture_strategy_verify CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 cargo test -p frankenengine-engine --test ecosystem_capture_strategy
